Happy Halloween: Smiley's Dog Costumes!
Our first event came unexpectedly, the Bridge and Tunnel Brewery was just five minute walk from our apartment but had costume! quickly cut out avocado out Dollar Tree cardboard paper and was success! Smiley won some toys and got growler for our fun last-minute costume.
You may have noticed the dog pictures Instagram, read the two posts wrote about our rescue Smiley you may have even read the viral Dodo article about his rescue story! Our unexpected adoption (it only took two weeks fostering realize that Smiley belonged with forever) changed all our plans. All those crazy Halloween parties wanted turned into going dog-friendly pub events, the dog parade and tonight we'll going the big NYC Halloween Parade with Smiley tow.
ended going another last minute event, the Tompkins Square dog parade, Smiley wore his same avocadog costume and got outshined popes and other crazy intricate creative costumes! was lot fun and we're definitely going again next year with better costume. Although most dogs wore hot dogs, delivery costumes and they rocked them, crafter got inspired something little more creative!
Tonight, the parade mainly for people but Smiley will hopefully steal the show long actually wears the costume made him and doesn't fall apart. Stay tuned for photos tonights shenanigans.
Author October 31, 2018 Categories Animals and Pets, New YorkTags Avocadog, Halloween, Halloween parade, Halloween party, New York, Smiley, TompkinsLeave comment Happy Halloween: Smiley's Dog Costumes!
Two days ago sent $300 adoption fee Pound Hounds Res-Q, the place that pulled Smiley from Brooklyn's high kill shelter, NYACC. had originally planned fostering Smiley, the cute year old pit mix who was abandoned because his former owners got pregnant. were position get dog don't make enough money and live small apartment, but couldn't let Smiley die.
ended adopting Smiley after just two weeks fostering, mostly because fell love with our foster boy and also because Pound Hounds Res-Q. addition the fee, also gave small donation because everything the rescue has done for us. Donating rescues helps them save more dogs encourage everyone the same!
The amazing thing about fostering, that it's practically free and saves lives! Shelters such the NYACC become overcrowded and put animals the kill list very quickly. They barely get chance get adopted! you foster dog, rescue will cover the vet bills and any necessary training. Then you help the dog get decompressed, preferably crate trained and then you help them find new forever home.
Fostering great way help animals without making lifetime commitment, that many people can't do. hadn't ended adopting Smiley, would have likely kept fostering, because has been rewarding. Let tell you bit about it!
Smiley went from the NYACC the vet get neutered, then special service was hired bring him us. had never met him even see him real life! Smiley was friendly and curious but wouldn't look make eye contact. also didn't respond commands, although were told that knew sit, stay and come.
When brought him inside, explored the place, sniffing every corner and demanding know what was behind every door. took over hour get him slow down and rest his doggy bed. lay there for while until tried put his harness for walk. wouldn't let put I'm and bared his teeth, backed off and let him sleep. few hours later, after husband came home, let put the harness without any fuss.
his first walk with us, pulled like crazy, giving rope burn. was strong and wouldn't listen all outside. immediately ordered front pulling harness, but got dragged around painfully for three whole days. also watched some videos how get dogs attention walks and tried implement them, which only half worked.
The first evening while watched TV, saw him watching from his bed. was the first time looked us. Not surprising after being handed off from one person another for ten days. was scared and confused.
crated him that first night instructed the rescue. People want adopt crate trained dogs and had try our best although didn't like the idea him being small cage. barked bit but quickly went sleep. The next morning woke wagging his tail us, was progress!
The second day acted like spoiled child, pushing his boundaries. would jump the sofa, demand treats and pulled even harder outside. may have had one two breakdowns that day because couldn't connect with him. couldn't see anyone adopting dog that was this crazy and also didn't know how long could spend with him, but didn't want disappoint him like humans have the past.
That night barked more his crate night, and was stressed knowing that would have leave him alone for six hours the next day. Everyone reassured that would okay, long wore cone was recently neutered and could rip his stitches.
When came home after our trip, that couldn't cancel although had wanted to, found his cone out shape and was practically hanging because piece got stuck his crate. was around his neck tight that coughed when cut off. seemed fine otherwise and extra friendly, but was traumatized the experience. was too scared putting him back the crate while had his cone, let him sleep our bedroom his bed.
When woke that Sunday (we had gotten him the afternoon Thursday) was completely different dog. was calm home, looked us, asked for pats and actually listened when gave him commands. That day took him beer festival because didn't want leave him home alone.
had been told that shouldn't around other dogs kids were extra careful. But was calm around kids, accepting treats gently and wagged his tail when saw other dogs. let him sniff few and seemed fine, were starting doubt everything that the kill shelter had said about him. found quiet spot the chaotic festival and sat with us, observing. People came over meet him and was friendly and loving everyone. one believed that had just rescued him.
The next day had received front clipping harness and the moment put him, stopped pulling walks. still got distracted outside but was much better walking calmly. let him sniff more dogs and discovered that was super friendly but couldn't tell between dogs that wanted play fight.
The following week started feel home. let him sit with the couch but still kept him out bed. behaved better every day. Except the one time jumped the bed when screamed because saw spider but that's because screamed and assume that was trying protect me.
During the week also let him play with some dogs while leashed. seemed get along with everyone, ignoring the dogs who were aggressive and backed away when angry cat jumped out him. was clearly good gentle boy!
That second weekend had him, took him amazing day care Petbuddy Services for trial day. were nervous see how would get long with other dogs but went really well! After day playing with dogs, was even calmer walks and less jumpy when met new dogs.
The day spent doggy daycare went out, but came home before picking Smiley up. Our home felt empty without him even though had only been there for ten days! That's when decided that we'd keeping him. But wanted wait case there were any issues with our landlord, although those were unlikely.
Wednesday took him the vet again, and even though the rescue knew were 99% likely adopt him, Pound Hounds Res-Q paid his bill. Later that evening, officially paid his adoption fee and became part our family. The next day, let him play off leash with bunch friendly dogs the park and did well, people didn't believe that was new rescue.
Since getting Smiley there has been more stress life need figure out where will while we're holiday. need worry about him getting sick, hurting himself feeling lonely when he's alone home. also have companion home now, smiling face that makes happier and can't walk him down the street without him getting compliments left and right. Oh, also gets out the house more and breathing that fresh New York air! I've even met few local dog owners that are quickly becoming friends.
course I'm already worrying about Smiley dying one day, but that's just how brain works. has already made life much better and more worthwhile. much think that everyone should adopt dog, five, immediately, understand that not everyone int position so.
Foster! you can't adopt. Donate rescues, you want help but can't foster. Dogs, and animals general, bring much happiness and unconditional love our hectic lives. They deserve our help and love, they should all feel safe and happy the same way they make feel. Please consider donating Pound Hounds Res-Q today, without them wouldn't have this beautiful dog part our family!
We've been Tonsai twice now once December and the second time July. December was hot, dry, lively and exciting! July was dead, everything was closed, the weather was miserable! Tonsai great place that's still pretty empty compared Phi Phi Island and even neighboring beaches.
While it's the perfect getaway during high (and dry) season, it's really not enjoyable during the low (monsoon) season. Unless you enjoy being one tourists hard get spot with only two available restaurants that mainly serve fried food. you come any other time, you can enjoy long tail boat ride the beach, choice many restaurants, food vendors and bars, there are lots people hanging out the beach the hostels but there are also thieving mischievous monkeys!
We're Fostering Dog Saved from the Kill Shelter!
Smiley was pulled from the NYACC Pound Hounds Res-Q. are considering adopting him ourselves because he's such good boy. The Dodo wrote about him week ago with the hopes that his story will inspire others adopt, foster and rescue dogs need!

The second time visited Thailand was the middle July, which right when monsoon season wreaking havoc the island and keeping all those selfie-taking tourists away but clearly not all them.
The first time visited Thailand was during the dry winter months when it's hot, overpopulated with tourists and low tied can ruin boat rides, kayaking and other water adventures. The second time came the middle July, which right when monsoon season wreaking havoc the island and keeping all those selfie-taking tourists away but clearly not all them.
week before arrived the famous case football camp boys who were stuck flooded cave and ferry had sunk, drowning many board. didn't know about this when booked and was quite scary there. got lucky and the weather ended clearing up, but all ferry and boat rides were rough, scary and puke-inducing.
Puffballs all sizes grow the forest, alongside roads, the middle green grassy lawn, they can really grow anywhere! You can stir fry them, cook them the oven and absolute favorite, pretending they are pancakes!
It's prime mushroom picking season but it's quickly cooling down. You might seeing mushrooms all around you, even city parks and the side highways! There's still some time out and forage before winter chases indoors for Netflix, hot chocolate and hibernation. However, most mushrooms take lot experience identify which can scary and discouraging.
also extremely dangerous eat anything that you're not certain about. Although there are many YouTube videos and Facebook groups are not always reliable way sure that you will safe. Even after you read this article, talk experienced mushroom pickers, join mycological society and always overly careful.
not legal pick mushrooms everywhere. Ask police officer, park ranger the info center where you can pick them. you anywhere else, you risk getting hefty fine. NYC can $250!
Wild mushrooms are not like the ones you buy the store. Some people might experience allergy specific type even though they are not allergic others. Some edible mushrooms have skins that certain people might react with stomach ache. Although lucky allergy free and tolerate everything I've tried far, I'm aware that might eat perfectly good edible mushroom that might make feel sick.
Let's get down business, there are mushrooms that are growing everywhere around New York right now and they happen taste delicious! The giant puffball (tiny ones are good too) really easy identify, grows huge it's satisfying find and it's great way begin your mushroom picking lifestyle.
Puffballs come round shapes and various sizes. They don't have any gills, stems, they grow right out the ground. The regular kind small, round, with tiny bumps and they get dark and dry when they are ready release their spores. When they are dry, you can stomp them and they will puff out dark-colored spores, which why they are called puffballs.
Giant puffballs don't taste any better than small ones, arguably they taste worse. But one big puffball can feed family three for day. They can grow larger than your head! their prime they are pure white the outside and inside.
they are any there color, have any markings, cautious.
Unlike the that wrote about earlier this week, giant puffballs have very poisonous lookalike, but it's extremely easy differentiate them! Once you pick puffball, cut down the middle from top bottom, edible puffball will have firm purely white firm flesh without any markings. should look like sliced mozzarella!
the mushroom off-white, it's edible kind that's past it's prime. Don't eat it! There are several stages puffball going bad. First, the inside will get yellower but will remain firm. Then it'll get wetter and darker, this point the outside might getting yellower too. Finally, dries and releases dark gray/black spores.
Even when puffball yellow, it's technically not poisonous. The only type puffball that poisonous, one with black lines that look like intricate designs. These will the outside and the inside. These puffballs are very poisonous and should NOT consumed.
 Just make sure cut every single mushroom half, and throw any puffball that isn't pure white the inside. The rule thumb with mushrooms is, there any doubts, leave behind.
you thought that the had many recipes, just wait until you bring home haul puffballs.
All you need cut 3/4 inch slices, fry them the oven the pan with some coconut oil and cinnamon and serve with maple syrup. you cook them long enough you can barely taste the difference they are soft the inside and crispy the outside. Delicious. Here are some other recipes.
Just quick reminder, some people don't react well the skin puffball mushrooms (or any mushroom skins). Some mushrooms have tough skin that's supposed protect the mushroom from being eaten, this skin can hard digest and some people are more sensitive than others.
It's very similar the skin certain nuts, you've ever pooped out intact almond, you know what I'm talking about. Personally, eat the skin but please aware that you might have reaction it! You can always start eating small piece with the skin on, wait few hours and see how you feel. can scary feel sick after eating wild mushroom and not know why.
you have any stories share about puffballs? What's your record find (size amount)? What's your favorite recipe? Share your puffball stories with the comments below!
find these mushrooms the most rewarding pick, because even small one enough side dish any meal and the bigger ones can feed entire family! Every time I've looked for hens, found least one and usually had 2-4 kilos (4-9 pounds) just minutes searching. course, you can also find single mushroom that weighs 13+ kilos (30+ pounds).
 The (maitake) doesn't have any poisonous look alikes, grows huge it's satisfying find and it's great way begin your mushroom picking lifestyle.
The looks little like the fluffy feathers hen, hence it's name. Here are some photos how they can look different stages growth.
Don't confuse the with the Chickens can grow higher the tree, they are orange and they grow layers. have heard lot about these mushrooms and I'm told they're delicious, however haven't found any yet and don't know how tell them apart from the many other orange mushrooms that grow trees! Stick with hens the beginning, you can't wrong with them and there are plenty around!
mushrooms typically grow oak trees, you see acorns the ground you're the right spot. you look the roots oak, you'll notice that they have clumpy shape with many little lumps which very similar the hen mushroom. From distance, you might confuse hen mushroom with some dried leaves. Always check out, and take look around the entire tree. They tend grow bigger, wider oaks and there can several clusters them around single tree!
are either off-white, kind beige/grayish more brownish, especially around it's rounded edges. They always grow clumps, from distance you could confuse them with clustered mushrooms remember that even though looks like cluster, it's one big mushroom that spreads out and looks fluffy. you cut the stem and you see many individual mushrooms, it's not hen. you see any gills (pictured below) NOT hen.
Hens are smooth, fluffy looking, single-stemmed mushrooms.
Remember, the picture above NOT It's example gills, that hens NOT have.
Sometimes, the grows trees other than oaks. you want extra safe, you can stick only eating the ones that you find oak trees, although like already said, there isn't any dangerous look-a-like. Hens are very unique looking.
Don't forget bring some common sense with you the forest. there are too many bugs the hen, looks dry off-color, then it's probably not good for eating. While most insects are pretty safe eat cooked correctly, you should stay away from unintentional entomophagy. Plus, insects can cause allergic reactions people who are also allergic shellfish.
Another common sense move avoid picking mushrooms forests where there's mark the tree, colorful rope tied around area. These could mark protected area, sick tree, some sort pest, pollution, disease, etc. looks questionable, don't there.
Same goes for roadside mushrooms. Think about the pollution that they are exposed to. Would you like some car exhaust with your mushrooms? don't think so. Don't pick anything that looks unclean (not including natural forest dirt) could polluted. Similarly road exhaust, some places could using pesticides other chemicals the area. Although most are already exposed them from the food buy supermarkets, the less chemicals consume, the better.
When you find hen and bring home, watch video how clean correctly. has many layers, the thick white stem isn't yummy the rest you want cut that off and possible, clean outside large tub avoid clogging your drain with forest debris. you see any holes, cut into them and remove any insects, spiders slugs.
How you cook hen?
There are many ways cook these mushrooms! Chop them into tiny pieces and stir fry, with other veggies, you're not vegetarian then maybe some meat, lard eggs make the perfect mushroomy omelette.
The nutritional value hens varies depending the website, but everyone agrees that they have very little fat protein and lots vitamin Some websites claim that they have very few carbs, others claim that they are 70% carbs. Almost everyone agrees that they are healthy addition balanced diet!
tend use too much olive oil and caramelized onions make fatty, crispy, scrumptious meal but there are much healthier alternatives. You can cook them the oven with coconut oil, you can steam them, boil them, grill them probably even air fry them. Whatever you chose do, make sure cook them well they can bit chewy and hard the stomach you undercook hens, although this true for mushrooms general. You can find various recipes here.
you didn't manage find any, didn't want risk picking the wrong mushroom, you can still enjoy eating wild Check out your local farmers markets from August until November and guarantee that you'll find some hens generally for per pound!
you enjoy picking mushrooms? Have you tried Share your stories, tips, recipes and favorite mushroom picking spots the comments below!

 They also had great happy hour deals which enjoyed and was interesting place visit. course, you couldn't film inside and only went quickly use the bathroom!
